notes: Held-out Material-design questions answerable on ANY stack. A pass routes to the correct component class + cites the neutral bundle page; it must stay technology-neutral (no <m3e-*> or framework markup).

cases.prompt: On a content screen I have one obviously-most-important action plus several secondary ones. In Material 3, which component class carries the primary action, and what's the mistake to avoid?
rubric: Selects the FAB (floating action button) for the single primary action; routes to /knowledge/components/fab.
Names the anti-pattern of using a FAB for non-primary actions; routes to /knowledge/anti-patterns/fab-for-non-primary-actions.
Answer is technology-neutral — no <m3e-*> tags, no framework code.
Cites the bundle by bundle-absolute path (starts with /knowledge/...).

cases.prompt: Is it fine to signal an input's error state with red color alone in a Material 3 UI?
rubric: Says no — color must not be the only signal; routes to /knowledge/anti-patterns/color-only-state-signaling and/or /knowledge/foundations/accessibility.
Mentions a non-color cue (icon, text, shape).
Stays technology-neutral; cites the bundle by /knowledge/... path.
